_comment_schema: alpha-001 recovery journal. Hand-edited by the founder per tester report. Each entry is one Resume click (right OR wrong). Empty array until cohort starts. Never imported from telemetry - no telemetry exists. See alpha/ALPHA_001_RUNBOOK.md for the cadence.

_field_spec.date: YYYY-MM-DD - the local date the tester clicked Resume
_field_spec.investigation: the title the launcher rendered on the recovery card (verbatim, the tester quotes it)
_field_spec.recovered: what was on the card: 'N tabs, M files, K-day gap'
_field_spec.accepted: true | false - did the tester click Resume?
_field_spec.wrong: true | false | null - did the reopened work match what they actually wanted? null = didn't click Resume
_field_spec.notes: one short sentence: what was useful, what was off, any quote

_invariants: Never store URLs, filenames, queries, or chat content. The 'investigation' field is the launcher title only.
A single row with wrong=true caps the trust dimension in readiness_score.compute() at 0.2.
If a tester reports a Resume that was right - log it. If they report nothing - leave them off; absence is not signal.
